turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having worsening abdominal pain and bloody stools for the past 3 weeks. The crampy lower abdominal pain is predominantly left-sided, and I've also noticed bright red blood mixed with stool when I have a bowel movement.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: The pain started off pretty mild at first, but it's gotten worse over time. It's crampy and feels like it's located mainly in my lower left abdomen. Sometimes it radiates to my back, but that's not constant. The bloody stools have been a real concern for me - they're bright red and mixed with mucus.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: I've also had some loose to watery stool with urgency, like I need to go really badly. And I've been experiencing fatigue that's affecting my work performance - it's getting worse with each passing day. I'm usually a pretty energetic person, but lately, I just feel exhausted all the time.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: I don't get sweaty or clammy during those episodes. To be honest, it's mostly the pain and fatigue that are bothering me right now.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: No, I don't get sweaty or clammy. It's really just the pain and fatigue that are my main concerns.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain is predominantly left-sided, but sometimes it radiates to my back. The sensation isn't exactly moving around; it's more like it's concentrated in that area with occasional episodes of back pain.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: The pain does radiate to my lower back, but I don't get any symptoms in my arms, neck, or jaw.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, there's no tingling or numbness in my arms. The pain and discomfort are mainly confined to my lower left abdomen and back.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, I don't get any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations in my extremities when I have these episodes. The discomfort is mainly limited to my lower abdomen and back.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling in my ankles, feet, or hands.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: No, my shoes don't feel tighter than usual, and there's no puffiness in my legs. The only symptom I've noticed is a bit of achy pain in both knees and ankles, but it's not related to swelling or redness - more like just general soreness when I'm active.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: I've had some nausea with the episodes, especially after eating. It's not constant, but it does happen from time to time. The loperamide hasn't really helped much with that either.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: Yes, I do get a bit nauseous during those episodes, especially after eating. It's not constant, but it does happen from time to time. The loperamide hasn't really helped much with that either. Mainly, though, it's the abdominal pain and fatigue that are bothering me right now.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've had a low-grade fever a few times per week - it's nothing too severe, but it's there. No chills or night sweats that I can recall, though my nighttime diarrhea has been pretty uncomfortable and is waking me up 2-3 times a night.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really had an increased appetite. If anything, it's probably decreased because of the nausea and discomfort after eating. And yeah, I have unintentionally lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past 3 weeks - it's been a concern for me, but I hadn't even thought to mention it until now.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: I've been working long hours at my software engineering job, often exceeding 60 hour weeks. Due to this, I've been eating mostly takeout and irregular meals. I don't really have time for a regular workout routine, but I do try to fit in some exercise when I can. As for my social life, I live alone in an apartment in Austin, Texas, so it's just me most of the time.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I'm a software engineer at a startup, and I've been working long hours, often exceeding 60 hour weeks. Due to my busy schedule, I don't really have time for a regular workout routine. I try to fit in some exercise when I can, but it's not consistent. Most days are spent staring at code or attending meetings, with little time for physical activity.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: Honestly, my diet has been pretty irregular due to my work schedule. I usually grab whatever is quick and easy, which tends to be takeout or fast food. Sometimes I'll have leftovers from last night's dinner if I'm lucky, but most days are just a bunch of processed foods and sugary snacks.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My father has type 2 diabetes, but my mother is healthy. As for siblings, my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which I've always found a bit concerning given our similar family background.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient:
My father has type 2 diabetes, and my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old. That's been a concern for me, especially since I've been experiencing these GI issues myself. 

(Note: The response only includes disclosed facts from G20 and G19)
